findings.evidence: `rg` shows the bundled API has `Cofree.lean:369: theorem cofree_toSemimoduleCat` and `Cofree.lean:376: theorem cofree_coact_tmul`, while analogous bundled APIs include `Regular.lean:139: theorem regular_coact` and finite/corestrict map-level coaction lemmas.
findings.fix: Add a `@[simp] theorem ComoduleCat.cofree_coact` stating the bundled coaction equals `(TensorProduct.assoc R M C C).symm.toLinearMap ∘ₗ Coalgebra.comul.lTensor M`, analogous to `ComoduleCat.regular_coact`.
findings.issue: `ComoduleCat.cofree` exposes only `cofree_toSemimoduleCat` and the pointwise `cofree_coact_tmul`; it has no bundled map-level lemma for `Comodule.coact (M := cofree R C M)`.

summary: The unbundled cofree structure now has the needed map-level coaction lemma, but the bundled `ComoduleCat.cofree` object still lacks the same characteristic equation. Since the bundled object is part of the advertised downstream API, users should not have to fall back through `of_coact`/unbundled unfolding or extensionalize over tensors to rewrite its coaction as a linear map.
